_meta.title: The Unusual Billionaires — Quantitative Specification Ledger
_meta.author: Saurabh Mukherjea (Ambit Capital, 2016)
_meta.purpose: Authoritative source of truth for all quantitative thresholds in the Greatness Formula. Used to verify correctness of fw_unusual_billionaires in core/scoring_engine.py and IBAS moat engine in core/data_engine.py.

greatness_formula._source_quote: revenue growth of 10 per cent and ROCE of 15 per cent every year for the past ten years

non_financial_companies._note_roce_derivation: ROCE = EBIT / Capital Employed. CE = Fixed Assets + Working Capital. Cost of capital for India is ~15% (8% risk-free + 6.5-7% equity risk premium). Companies must exceed their own cost of capital to be great.

financial_companies_bfsi._note: BFSI companies use ROE and loan growth instead of ROCE and revenue (banks have no traditional ROCE). The proxy in code uses revenue CAGR since loan book data is not in CSV — this is the best available approximation.
financial_companies_bfsi._source_quote: ROE of 15 per cent and loan book growth of 15 per cent every year for financial services companies

cost_of_capital_derivation._note: 8% = 10-year GOI bond yield (early 2016). ERP = 4% US long-term ERP + 2.5% India rating premium (S&P BBB-). Sum = ~15%. Validated by Sensex 20/30-year CAGR of ~15%.

roce_10y.proxy_justification: 10Y ROCE median covers the full decade window. Two overlapping windows (10Y + 5Y both clearing) is harder to fake than a single average.

roce_5y.book_requirement: ROCE ≥ 15% still intact (moat must be current)
roce_5y.proxy_justification: 5Y ROCE median confirms moat has not eroded in the recent window.

revenue_growth_non_fin.book_requirement: Revenue growth ≥ 10% every year for 10 consecutive years
revenue_growth_non_fin.proxy_justification: 10Y CAGR covers the full period. Individual year checks (y2–y5) enforce per-year consistency for the 4 observable annual windows. Years 6-10 covered only by CAGR due to data limitation.

revenue_growth_financial.book_requirement: Loan book growth ≥ 15% every year for 10 consecutive years
revenue_growth_financial.proxy_justification: Loan book data not available in CSV. Revenue CAGR used as best available proxy. The 15% hurdle matches the book's loan growth threshold.

roe_financial.proxy_justification: For financial companies, ROE and ROCE computed from CSV data are often similar. Dedicated ROE 10Y median column would be more accurate but is not available.

year_by_year_revenue.book_requirement: Revenue ≥ 10% per year individually (not just as CAGR average)
year_by_year_revenue.code_proxy: _cc_each_year: rev_gr_y2 >= 10 AND rev_gr_y3 >= 10 AND rev_gr_y4 >= 10 AND rev_gr_y5 >= 10

opm_stability.proxy_justification: opm_stable is a pre-computed flag (1 if OPM standard deviation < threshold across years). Best available proxy for margin resilience.

debt_discipline.book_requirement: D/E < 1.0 (from Codex chapter on capital discipline)

ibas_moat_framework._critical_finding: IBAS framework is ENTIRELY QUALITATIVE in the book. There are NO quantitative thresholds specified for any of the four moat types. The proxies below are engineering implementations derived from financial theory.

brands_and_reputation.book_definition: Reputation allows firms to charge premium prices for quality customers cannot verify before purchase. Built over years; costly to replicate.
brands_and_reputation.proxy_rationale: Stable OPM = ability to maintain pricing power (brand moat). NPM above own 5Y median = premium sustained over time. Both together = 100.

architecture.book_definition: Network of formal and informal contracts with employees, suppliers, customers. Allows firms with ordinary people to produce extraordinary results.
architecture.book_example: GCMMF (Amul), Maruti Suzuki supply chain — 270 suppliers integrated into JIT delivery
architecture.proxy_rationale: Negative Cash Conversion Cycle = company collects from customers before paying suppliers (HUL, Asian Paints, Nestle pattern). This is the financial signature of dominant supply chain bargaining power — Kay's 'relational contract' moat in quantifiable form.

innovation.book_definition: Source of competitive advantage but most tenuous — expensive, uncertain, hard to manage. Powerful when twinned with reputation and architecture.
innovation.book_caveat: Innovation is rarely a sustainable standalone moat; it must be protected by brand and architecture.
innovation.proxy_rationale: NFAT > 4 = asset-light model generating 4× revenue per unit of fixed assets (capital efficiency = innovation in process). SSGR self-funded = growth funded from internal cash without dilution (innovation that creates value rather than destroys it).

strategic_assets.book_definition: Intellectual property (patents), licences, natural resource access, political contacts, sunk-cost first-mover advantages, natural monopolies.
strategic_assets.proxy_rationale: d19 = change in net fixed assets. Positive d19 means the company is actively converting CWIP to productive capacity — capital is flowing into real strategic infrastructure, not sitting idle. Best available proxy for asset-base moat without balance sheet line-by-line data.

interpretation.0-24: Commodity business with no identifiable moat

chapter_10_checklist._chapter: Chapter 10 — A Checklist for Long-term Investors
chapter_10_checklist._finding: Chapter 10 is ENTIRELY QUALITATIVE. Three categories: (1) Industry Attractiveness, (2) Management Quality, (3) Competitive Advantages. No new quantitative thresholds beyond the 15% ROCE cost of capital reference.

quantitative_anchors._note: Industry average ROCE vs 15% cost of capital is the only quantitative check. Sectors with average ROCE > 15% viewed positively.

confirmed_correct_in_production: IBAS moat engine (4-factor model in data_engine.py)
Financial ROE threshold in fw_coffee_can (>= 15, not 12)
Revenue 2-5 Years Back added to COLUMN_MAP
year-by-year rev_gr_y2..y5 computation loop in data_engine.py

gaps_and_limitations.gap: Loan book growth not available in CSV — revenue used as proxy for financial companies
gaps_and_limitations.impact: Financial companies evaluated on revenue CAGR, not loan book CAGR. Will under-flag banks with stable revenues but declining loan books.

gaps_and_limitations.gap: Individual year ROCE check not implemented (only 10Y and 5Y medians)
gaps_and_limitations.impact: A company with volatile ROCE (15%, 5%, 25%, 15%...) could pass the median check but fail the strict per-year requirement. No ROCE-by-year data in CSV.
gaps_and_limitations.mitigation: Two overlapping ROCE median windows (10Y + 5Y) provide redundant coverage.

gaps_and_limitations.gap: Years 6-10 individual revenue consistency not checkable
gaps_and_limitations.impact: CSV provides Revenue 2-5 Years Back. Years 6-10 verified only by 10Y CAGR average.
gaps_and_limitations.mitigation: _cc_each_year covers years 2-5 individually; CAGR covers the remaining window.

gaps_and_limitations.gap: IBAS moat proxies are approximations, not direct measurements
gaps_and_limitations.impact: A company with strong architecture but positive CCC (e.g., engineering firm with long payment cycles) would score 0 on architecture despite having genuine Kay moat.
gaps_and_limitations.mitigation: Documented in code comments. IBAS is intended as a ranking signal, not a binary gate.
